NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO INSPECTION DEMANDS AND FORM INTERROGATORY AND FOR SANCTIONS, CALENDAR OF DEC-18-2020 - NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER AND TRUSTEE CARLYCE INGRAM'S ("PETITIONER") REQUEST TO CONTINUE THIS HEARING IS DENIED. OBJECTORS DEARIES INGRAM AND ADRIENNE INGRAM' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO INSPECTION DEMANDS AND FORM INTERROGATORY IS GRANTED. OBJECTORS' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. FORMER COUNSEL LEE SHALL PAY TO OBJECTORS $1,840 WITHIN 10 DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER AND CURRENT COUNSEL SIMON SHALL PAY TO OBJECTORS $920 WITHIN 10 DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER (FOR A TOTAL SANCTIONS AWARD OF $2,760). OBJECTORS SHALL PREPARE AND LODGE WITH THE COURT CLERK IN DEPARTMENT 204 A PROPOSED ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THIS TENTATIVE RULING. (SEE TENTATIVE RULING FOR DETAILS)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial
Off Calendar May-11-2021 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Confirming Trust, Successor Trustee, And Transfer Of Assets
Off Calendar May-11-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Simon, personal appearance, counsel for Carlyce Ingram, Petitioner; Christopher Land, via ZOOM, counsel for Adrienne Ireton and Dearies Ingram, Objectors.

Mr. Simon made a personal appearance for the purpose of having the Court sign an order regarding settlement. The Court noted that the settlement agreement requires filing of a dismissal at some point and questions what Court approval means to Counsel. Mr. Simon stated that the parties have agreed to a stipulated order to transfer the property and setting aside the deed. Mr. Simon also stated that the tax implications from the 2013 trust are fairly significant, that the County Assessor considers it not to be a transfer from parent to child, and that they are seeking the Court's blessing. The Court signed the order approving the settlement/essentially approving Heggstad and voiding the affidavit-death of Trustee. Mr. Simon is to monitor the Court's website and serve a copy of the order on all parties.

The Petition for Order Confirming Trust, etc. and the trial are off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Reconsideration; Memo Of Points And Authorities; Declaration
Off Calendar Mar-25-2021 Text Ruling

Motion For Reconsideration; Memo Of Points And Authorities; Declaration
Off Calendar Mar-25-2021 Text Ruling

Petition For Order Confirming Trust, Successor Trustee, And Transfer Of Assets
Off Calendar Jan-12-2021 Continued To May-11-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Simon appeared by zoom for Carlyce Ingram (Petitioner/Successor Trustee).
Christopher Land appeared by zoom for Adrienne Ireton and DeAries Ingram (Objectors).

Mr. Land stated that there has been no development towards settlement and that he is ready to go to trial. Mr. Simon stated that the Petitioner recommends going back to mediation. Mr. Land stated that his Clients are not interested in going back to mediation. The Court noted that the parties have already gone to pro bono mediation and stated that it cannot order it again, strongly recommends that the parties go back to Ms. Epstein for mediation and informed Counsel that it will be issuing a case management order. Mr. Simon raised the issue of having the removal issue heard first before going to trial. The Court stated that a petition for removal is not set yet and that it will set a trial date. Mr. Simon stated that his Client is living in a property, that Mr. Land filed a notice of death in the first trust, that the first trust gives the property to three grandchildren, that it raised the property taxes and that the expenses are being paid by his Client. Mr. Land stated that they are required to provide notification of death. Mr. Simon asked the Court to set the trial 60-days out and stated that he needs to depose the opposing party. Discussion was held as to when the trial date should be set. The Petition for Order Confirming Trust, etc. is continued to May 11, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The trial date is also set for May 11, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Counsel that it will look at the trial plan and determine if the trial will remain in Probate or be sent out to the Trial Department. Counsel for the Petitioner is to monitor the Court's register of actions. The Court informed Counsel that the order will direct the Petitioner to serve the order.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order Confirming Trust, Successor Trustee, And Transfer Of Assets
Off Calendar Mar-10-2020 Continued To May-19-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elaine Lee for Petitioner.
Christopher Land for DeAries Ingram and Adrienne Ireton.

Ms. Lee stated that she would like the Court to set a trial date and refer them to pro bono mediation. Mr. Land stated that he agrees to mediation, but they have two unrepresented Beneficiaries and he does not know if they will participate in Court ordered mediation. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than May 12, 2020. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by May 14, 2020. The Petition for Order Confirming Trust, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on May 19, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The Court informed Ms. Lee that it will not set a trial date at this time.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

Petition For Order Confirming Trust, Successor Trustee, And Transfer Of Assets
Off Calendar Feb-11-2020 Continued To Mar-10-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Inspection Demands And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-05-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Elaine Lee, Esq. for and with Carlyce Ingram.
John A. Kithas, Esq. for DeAries Ingram, et al.

Matter called. Argument is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on Further Responses To Inspection Demands And For Sanctions as follows: The motion to compel is granted in part. No timely opposition was filed. Objectors are entitled to a response to the request for production separately addressing each of the 66 requests. Given the history of the communications leading up to the response to the request for production, objections are not deemed waived. Objectors are awarded $1260 as reasonable sanctions because Petitioner was not substantially justified in her response to the discovery request.

Petitioner shall deliver the ordered responses and sanctions payment to Objector's counsel within 15 days. (Order signed signed in open court.)

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

Petition For Order Confirming Trust, Successor Trustee, And Transfer Of Assets
Off Calendar Jan-14-2020 Continued To Feb-11-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Confirming Trust, Successor Trustee, And Transfer Of Assets
Off Calendar Nov-12-2019 Continued To Jan-14-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elaine Lee for and with Carlyce Ingram (Petitioner).
Christopher Land for and with DeAries Ingram and Adrienne Ireton (Objectors).

Ms. Lee stated that since the last hearing her Client has made two offers, that both of the offers were rejected, that they received a request for documents and that she has provided records from the fiscal Conservator. Ms. Lee also stated that her Client has no access to financial information, requested that the Objectors accept what was approved by the Court at the last fiscal conservatorship hearing and that the Court rule that there is to be no further discovery. The Court noted that it has nothing before it with respect to Ms. Lee's request. Mr. Land stated that he received some information that makes reference to 14 accounts, that the Conservator only listed two accounts, that there is a lot of room for missing information, that Ms. Lee indicated that she did not receive their request, that she then refused to produce documents and that he may have to move to compel/file motions. The Court reminds Counsel that if it receives a declaration indicating that they do not have documents to produce he may lose his motion. Ms. Lee stated that she would like to move on mediation. The Court encouraged Counsel to work cooperatively in providing information and towards resolution. The Petition for Order Confirming Trust, etc. is continued to January 14, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to meet and confer in good faith to make a determination that everything that could be provided has been provided.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order Confirming Trust, Successor Trustee, And Transfer Of Assets
Off Calendar Aug-13-2019 Continued To Nov-12-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Elaine Ruth Lee for and with Carlyce Ingram. John Kithas and Christopher Land for and with DeAries Ingram (Objector) and also appearing for Adrienne Ireton (Objector) who was not present. Philip Ireton, Adrienne Ireton's Husband, was present. Carlyce Ingram's Son "Carlyce" was also present.

The Court stated that there is a dispute whether the later trust revoked the earlier trust. Ms. Lee stated that she had spoken with the objectors and that it appears that the issues will need to be litigated. Mr. Kithas stated that his client's are open to mediation and that the matter will be ready to mediate in four to five months. Ms. Lee presented opposition stating that matters were not submitted in a timely fashion. The Court stated that the parties "are where they are" and that the Court needs to know what needs to be done or happen so the matter can get to mediation or trial. Mr. Kithas stated that the decedent passed away one and a half years ago, that there's been no account, that discovery needs to be done, and that there are questions about Carlyce Ingram's handling of his Mother's estate. Ms. Lee presented brief argument stating that the Heggstad petition has now morphed into something else. The Court inquired about what would done by the parties going forward to either have the matter ready for mediation or set for trial. Brief discussions regarding procedural issues were held. The Court stated that future filings must be properly noticed. Ms. Lee stated that they did file the 1994 Trust. At the conclusion of argument/discussions, the Court continued the Petition for Order Confirming Trust, Successor Trustee, and Transfer of Assets to November 12, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that a joint status statement is to be on file by November 5, 2019. The joint status statement should state where things are going and what efforts were made by the parties up to that point.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

Petition For Order Confirming Trust, Successor Trustee, And Transfer Of Assets
Off Calendar Jul-09-2019 Continued To Aug-13-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Confirming Trust, Successor Trustee, And Transfer Of Assets
Off Calendar Jun-04-2019 Continued To Jul-09-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elaine Lee for and with Carlyce Ingram.
Adriene Ireton, Dearies Ingram, Darnell Ingram, Lakesha Ingram and Carlyce Ahmal Ingram (all present in Propria Persona).

Adriene Ireton and Dearies Ingram stated that they are objecting to the petition. The Petition for Order Confirming Trust, etc. is continued to July 9, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 so that the objections can be put in writing, filed and served on everybody so that they can be dealt with. The objections are to be filed no later than July 3, 2019. The Court strongly encourages the parties to get an Attorney, stated that it does not have a copy of the 1994 trust in order to determine that all of the Beneficiaries were provided with notice, and that if the Petitioner intends to have the earlier trust revoked, it requires an amendment. Ms. Lee stated that she has an amendment and that she can provide a copy of the 1994 trust.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
